heading: Floating dry docks
label: 55122 Floating dry docks

children.label: A
children.text: is being used to launch or raise a vessel in connection with the construction, maintenance, or repair of that vessel;

children.label: i
children.text: a shipyard located in the United States that is an eligible owner specified under ; orsection 12103(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an affiliate of such a shipyard; and

children.label: i
children.text: was owned or contracted for purchase by such shipyard or affiliate prior to the date of the enactment of the Carl Levin and Howard P. “Buck” McKeon National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2015; or

children.label: ii
children.text: had a letter of intent for purchase by such shipyard or affiliate signed prior to such date of enactment; and

the movement occurs within 5 nautical miles of the shipyard or affiliate that owns and operates such floating dry dock or, in the case of a dry dock described in paragraph (1)(C)(ii), occurs between Honolulu, Hawaii, and Pearl Harbor, Hawaii.

children.heading: Dry Docks for Construction of Certain Naval Vessels.—
children.label: b Dry Docks for Construction of Certain Naval Vessels.—
A
children.text: such dry dock is necessary for the timely completion of such construction; and

children.label: I
children.text: a shipyard located in the United States that is an eligible owner specified under section 12103(b); or

children.label: II
children.text: an affiliate of such a shipyard; or

children.label: I
children.text: owned by the State in which the shipyard is located or a political subdivision of that State; and

children.label: II
children.text: operated by a shipyard located in the United States that is an eligible owner specified under section 12103(b).

children.heading: .—Notice to congress
children.label: 2 .—Notice to congress
children.text: Not later than 30 days after making a determination under paragraph (1), the Secretary of the Navy shall notify the Committee on Armed Services and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Armed Services and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate of such determination.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: c .—Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “floating dry dock” means equipment with wing walls and a fully submersible deck.
